items.title: 1. COLETILLAS
items.content_es:
Coletilla (EN) ¿Qué significa? (ES) ¿Para qué se usa en el reading?
As indicated in the text, … Como se indica en el texto…
Para introducir un dato literal o muy claro del texto.
According to the text, … Según el texto… Para responder directamente con información del texto (la más típica).
Based on the text, … Basado en el texto… Para respuestas que implican deducir/explicar a partir del texto, sin inventar.
As explained by the (reading/text), …
Como explica la lectura/el texto…
Para explicar una idea del texto con tus palabras (paráfrasis).
The text indicates that … El texto indica que… Para señalar una conclusión clara del texto.
In the text, it says that … En el texto, se dice que…
Para “citar” la idea sin copiar frases exactas.
According to the text/reading/passage, …
Según el texto/lectura/pasaje…
Igual que “According to the text”, pero variando “text/reading/passage”.
As maintained by the text, …
Como sostiene el texto…
Cuando el texto afirma/defiende una idea (más formal).
As reported by the text, … Como informa el texto…
Estilo “informe”: ideal para hechos/actuaciones.
From my point of view, these words mean …
Desde mi punto de vista, estas palabras significan…
Para definir vocabulario (si el ejercicio lo pide).
What these words imply is …
Lo que estas palabras implican es…
Para explicar el sentido/idea de una expresión.

items.title: Después De Las Coletillas:
items.content_es:
Función Plantilla (EN) para pegar tras la coletilla
Cómo usarla (ES) Ejemplo B1 (EN)
Causa → resultado
This happened because + causa, so + resultado.
1) Di por qué pasó. 2) Di qué consecuencia tuvo.
This happened because they left in a hurry, so the guard decided to follow them through the centre.
Orden de hechos
First, … then, … finally, …
Ideal cuando el texto cuenta pasos/acciones. Usa 3 mini-hechos en orden.
First the guard noticed them, then another guard stopped them, and finally the police arrived and checked the situation.
Problema → explicación
The issue was that + problema. This meant that + efecto.
Perfecta para preguntas tipo “what caused… / what was the consequence…”.
The issue was that their stories were different. This meant that the police were unsure who was telling the truth.
Duda / cautela It seems that … because …
Para no “inventar”: describes una impresión basada en un dato del texto.
It seems that the accusation was unclear at first because the boy said he already had the hat when he entered.
Contraste (pero…)
… However, … Úsala cuando hay dos partes opuestas (acusación vs explicación; calma vs tensión).
They said they did not steal it. However, the guard claimed the hat belonged to the shop.
Concesión (aunque…)
Although + idea 1, + idea 2.
Para sonar natural en B1: “aunque X, pasó Y”.
Although they defended their version, the situation became tense and the officers escorted everyone out.
Acción policial + propósito
The officers + Past Simple + to + objetivo.
Muy “policial”: acción + para qué. Ojo: to + infinitive.
The officers checked the hat to confirm the facts and avoid a bigger argument in the backroom.

Prueba / detalle clave
A key detail is that … which + impacto.
Para preguntas que piden “important observation / impact”.
A key detail is that the boys were wearing the hat when they entered, which weakened the accusation of theft.
Habla/declara (reported speech)
The guard claimed that … / The boy insisted that …
Sirve para resumir declaraciones sin copiar comillas.
The guard claimed that they stole the hat, but the boy insisted that it was his and he had it before entering.
Resultado final As a result, … Para cerrar con consecuencia clara.
As a result, the assistant manager’s comment changed the situation and the officers reconsidered the accusation.

A: He has provided his ID. N: He hasn’t provided his ID. Q: Has he provided his ID?
⚠ Trampas típicas de tipo test (Present Perfect)
- Se usa para acciones que empezaron en el pasado y continúan ahora.
- Estructura: have / has + participio.
- for = duración (two years, three months).
- since = punto concreto en el pasado (2020, Monday).
- ago exige Past Simple, nunca Present Perfect.
- En preguntas de duración, la estructura correcta es: How long + have/has + sujeto
+
been…?
- Si la acción ya terminó, usamos Past Simple, aunque aparezca for.
- Si la situación sigue vigente, usamos Present Perfect.

- EXTRA When + pasado (preguntas)

items.title: Ejemplos:
items.content_es:
- Radium was discovered by Pierre and Marie Curie.
- The Lord of the Rings was written by J.R.R. Tolkien.
- Paper was invented by the Chinese.
EXTRA — Grammar de examen (jurídico)
Relative clauses
Expresión / estructura
Uso (ES) Ejemplo correcto (EN)
who para personas The man who robbed the bank was arrested.
where para lugares That is the place where the burglary happened.
when para momentos / tiempo
That was the moment when the suspect fled.

whose posesión The witness whose car was stolen called the police.
which / that para cosas The evidence which was presented in court was convincing.
Formal / legal grammar
Estructura Uso (ES) Ejemplo correcto (EN)
Had + subject + past participle
condicional formal / inversión
Had the defendant appeared in court on time, the trial could have started earlier.
recommend / suggest / order / insist / it is essential that + base verb
subjuntivo formal jurídico
The defense attorney recommended that his client remain silent.
deny + -ing negar haber hecho algo
He denied stealing the car.
by the time + Past Simple / Past Perfect
una acción ya había pasado antes de otra
The thief had already escaped by the time the police arrived.
if + had had trampa típica de tercer condicional
If the suspect had had an alibi, he might not have been convicted.
at midnight hora exacta The robbery was committed at midnight.
pieces of evidence + plural verb
evidencia en partes contables
Several pieces of evidence were submitted during the trial.
Regla rápida
- Had + participio = forma más formal de una condicional.
- Tras recommend / suggest / order / essential, usa verbo base.
- deny va con -ing.
- evidence suele ser incontable, pero pieces of evidence va en plural.
- at midnight, no in midnight.

A: S + am/is/are + V-ing · N: S + am/is/are not + V-ing · Q: Am/Is/Are + S + V-ing?
Horario oficial Present Simple (future)
A: S + V(s) · N: S + don’t/doesn’t + V · Q: Do/Does + S + V?
Decisión rápida / promesa
Will A: S + will + V · N: S + won’t + V · Q: Will + S + V?
Intención / predicción
Going to A: S + am/is/are going to + V · N: S + am/is/are not going to + V · Q: Am/Is/Are + S + going to + V?
- Plan ya decidido — Present Continuous (future) A: We are leaving tomorrow morning. N: We aren’t leaving tomorrow morning. Q: Are we leaving tomorrow morning? ● Horario oficial — Present Simple (future) A: The train leaves at 7:45. N: The train doesn’t leave at 7:45. Q: Does the train leave at 7:45? ● Decisión rápida / promesa — Will A: I will take a taxi. N: I won’t take a taxi. Q: Will I take a taxi? ● Intención / predicción — Going to A: He is going to report the incident. N: He isn’t going to report the incident. Q: Is he going to report the incident?

items.section: Collocations / Idioms / Fixed Expressions / False Friends
items.title: FRIENDS
items.content_es:
- Collocations = palabras que van juntas naturalmente. ● Idioms = expresiones de significado figurado. ● Fixed expressions = estructuras fijas que no conviene cambiar. ● False friends = palabras que parecen español, pero significan otra cosa.
Collocation ● Verb + Noun (o Verb + Noun phrase) Collocation (EN) Significado (ES) ❌ Típico mal
*make an arrest efectuar una detención do an arrest
*place someone under arrest
poner a alguien bajo arresto make someone under arrest
commit a crime cometer un delito make a crime
carry out an investigation
llevar a cabo una investigación
do an investigation
launch an inquiry iniciar una investigación (muy formal)
make an inquiry
*gather evidence recopilar pruebas gather proofs / collect evidences
*question a suspect interrogar a un sospechoso question to a suspect
*file a report redactar un informe make a report
*press charges presentar cargos put charges
*open / close a case abrir / cerrar un caso open the crime
*patrol an area patrullar una zona patrol in the area (lo típico es que falte el “an area”)
*respond to an incident
responder a un incidente respond an incident (sin to)
*use reasonable force
usar fuerza proporcional use reasonable forces (plural)

break the law infringir la ley destroy the law

give evidence declarar como prueba say evidence
serve a sentence cumplir condena do a sentence
bend the law “doblar” la ley (interpretarla de forma laxa)
break the law (no es lo mismo: es ya infringirla)
take a risk asumir un riesgo do a risk
make a decision tomar una decisión do a decision
make a mistake cometer un error do a mistake
Receive treatment Recibir tratamiento (médico) Take treatment (not commonly used this way)

contrainterrogar a un testigo examine again a witness

imponer cadena perpetua give down a sentence

alcanzar un veredicto unánime
make a unanimous verdict

have many frauds

iniciar una búsqueda nacional make a manhunt
hear the evidence in court
oír las pruebas en juicio listen the evidence

reunir / conectar todos los hechos
join all the facts badly

recibir asesoramiento de un experto
take an advice from an expert

entrar forzando para robar
The thieves broke into the shop.
break into, no break in the shop

el off forma parte de la expresión

cumplir las normas

comply with, no comply the rules

charged with, no charged of

declararse no culpable de todos los cargos

plead not guilty to, no plead not guilty of

no significa without evidence

convicted of, no convicted for

finded guilty / founded guilty

on probation, no in probation

items.prompt: 5. LINKING WORDS (Conectores) ● Timeline Conector Significado Estructura ✅ Ejemplo correcto ❌ Típico mal
items.content_es:
First, Primero, First, + oración
First, the alarm rang. First the alarm rang (sin coma; en examen mejor con coma)
Then, Luego, Then, + oración
Then, the guard heard a loud noise.
Then the guard he heard… (doble sujeto / estructura rara)
After that,
Después, After that, + oración
After that, he saw the thief.
After that he saw… (sin coma; menos limpio)
Finally, Finalmente, Finally, + oración
Finally, the suspect escaped through the back door.
Finally the suspect was escape… (error de verbo)
- Reading
Cause (causa) - because = porque Estructura: because + oración Ejemplo correcto: The guard followed the boys because they left the shop in a hurry. Típico error: ❌ because of they left the shop in a hurry - since = ya que / puesto que Estructura: since + oración Ejemplo correcto: The machine was harder to break since it relied on more complex codes. Típico error: ❌ since more complex codes - as = como / ya que Estructura: as + oración Ejemplo correcto: The system was stronger as it used more advanced codes. Típico error: ❌ as use more advanced codes - due to = debido a Estructura: due to + nombre / grupo nominal Ejemplo correcto: Crime decreased due to increased patrolling. Típico error: ❌ due to crime decreased

items.title: 6. Situaciones funcionales resuelta
items.content_es:
Situación Cómo responder en inglés Qué entrenas realmente
Queja por ruido en apartamento turístico
We have received a noise complaint from other residents. You need to reduce the volume immediately. This rule is in place to protect public order and respect the neighbours’ rest. If the noise continues, it may result in a fine. Please
advertencia formal + motivo + consecuencia + cierre policial

cooperate and keep the noise down from now on.
Entrevista breve a testigo de un robo
Can you tell me what happened? What did the suspect look like? What was he wearing? Did you see which direction he went? Were there any other witnesses or CCTV cameras nearby? Please stay here for a moment, as your statement may help the investigation.
preguntas funcionales + descripción + dirección + cierre policial
Objeto sospechoso abandonado en zona pública
Please step back and do not touch the bag. We need to keep the area clear until officers check the situation. This is for public safety. I’m going to cordon off the area and call the appropriate unit. Please follow my instructions and remain at a safe distance.
instrucciones claras + seguridad + imperativo + lenguaje policial
Vehículo bloqueando acceso de emergencia
You cannot park here because this entrance must remain clear for emergency vehicles. This rule is in place to protect public safety. You need to move the car immediately. If you do not comply, it could result in a fine or the vehicle may be removed.
prohibición + motivo de seguridad + obligación inmediata + consecuencia

items.title: Preguntas De Examen
items.content_es:
Pregunta de Examen
- EXPLICACIÓN DE TÉRMINOS Expresión Idea (ES) Model answer (EN, B1)
* be under arrest estar detenido What this expression implies is that the person is officially detained by the police.
* take someone into custody
poner bajo custodia / detener
It means the police detain the person and take control of the situation.
* be caught red-handed
pillado in fraganti It means the person is caught while committing the crime.
* book someone fichar oficialmente It means the police officially arrest and register the person.
* get away with it salir impune It means someone did something wrong but was not punished.
* crack the case resolver el caso It means the police solve the case and identify the offender.
* follow a lead seguir una pista It means the police continue the investigation using new information.
* gather evidence reunir/recopilar pruebas
It means officers collect proof to support what happened.
* question / interrogate a suspect
interrogar a un sospechoso
It means the police ask the suspect questions to clarify the facts.
* file a report redactar/presentar informe
It means making an official report about the incident.
* press charges presentar cargos It means starting legal action against a person for a crime.
* break the law infringir la ley It means doing something illegal. * summons citación judicial oficial It means an official order to appear in court.
* uncover destapar / descubrir algo oculto
It means discovering something hidden, such as a criminal network.

* bend the law interpretar la ley de forma laxa para beneficiarse
It means using the law in a flexible way to gain an advantage without openly breaking it.
* actual real / verdadero (no “actual”)
It means real or true, not current.
* crime delito en general It means an act that is punishable by law.
* offense infracción / violación de la ley (término amplio)
It means a general violation of the law.
* felony delito grave It means a serious crime, such as murder or robbery.
* misdemeanor delito menor / falta It means a minor crime or offense.
wrongdoing acto ilegal o inmoral It means an illegal or immoral act, not always a serious crime.
* evidence evidencia / información que prueba hechos
It means information that helps prove facts in a case.
* proof prueba concluyente / confirmación clara
It means clear confirmation that something is true.
* clues pistas It means objects or facts that help solve a crime.
exhibits pruebas físicas presentadas en juicio
It means physical evidence presented in court.
* prison prisión tras condena / largo plazo
It means a place where convicted criminals serve long sentences.
* jail cárcel preventiva / corto plazo
It means a place for short-term detention or for people waiting for trial.
penitentiary término formal para prisión
It means a formal word for prison, especially in legal contexts.
correctional facility término institucional/oficial para prisión
It means an official institutional term for prison.
investigate investigar formalmente It means carrying out a formal police investigation.

look into examinar un caso o situación
It means examining a case or situation to find out more.
probe investigar de manera profunda
It means investigating something deeply and thoroughly.
launch an inquiry iniciar oficialmente una investigación
It means officially starting an investigation.
- ENTREVISTAS Objetivo Pregunta base (EN) Alternativa útil (EN)
Nombre completo What’s your full name? Can you tell me your full name?
Apellido What’s your surname? What’s your last name?
Deletrear Can you spell your surname, please?
Could you spell that, please?
Fecha de nacimiento What’s your date of birth? When were you born?
Edad How old are you? What is your age?
Nacionalidad What’s your nationality? Where are you from?
Dirección What’s your address? Where do you live?
Código postal What’s the postcode? Could you confirm the postcode?
Teléfono What’s your phone number? What’s your mobile number?
Documento Do you have an ID card or passport?
Can I see your ID, please?
Contacto de emergencia
Who should we contact in an emergency?
Emergency contact details?

- Direcciones Expresión / pregunta Uso (ES) Ejemplo de uso (EN)
Go straight ahead. seguir recto Go straight ahead for two minutes.
Turn left / right. girar a la izquierda / derecha
Turn right at the traffic lights.
At the roundabout, take the second exit.
indicar salida en rotonda
At the roundabout, take the second exit.
Go past / walk past pasar de largo Go past the supermarket and keep walking.
It’s on your left / right. indicar posición final It’s on your left, next to the bank.
Next to / opposite / in front of / behind
dar referencias It’s opposite the pharmacy.
You’ve gone too far. indicar que se ha pasado
If you reach the roundabout again, you’ve gone too far.
- Estado físico / Asistencia Paso Pregunta / orden (EN) Uso (ES)
1 Are you injured? comprobar si hay lesión
2 Where does it hurt? localizar el dolor
3 Are you bleeding? comprobar sangrado
4 Can you breathe normally? comprobar respiración
5 Are you feeling dizzy? comprobar mareo
6 Do you need medical assistance? valorar ayuda médica
7 Please stay calm. tranquilizar
8 Don’t move. evitar empeorar la lesión
9 Sit down, please. estabilizar
10 I’m calling an ambulance. informar de la actuación

- Descripción Física / Ropa Pregunta Uso
What does he/she look like? pedir descripción general
What was he/she wearing? preguntar por la ropa
Any distinctive features? pedir rasgos distintivos
Bloque Vocabulario útil
Altura tall / short
Complexión slim / medium build / overweight
Pelo short hair / long hair / blond / brown / black

items.title: READING 1 — MURDER MYSTERY
items.content_es:
Once, when I was teaching English to children in Cambridge, I died while eating my dinner.
The children were eleven to fourteen years old and there were about one hundred and
twenty
of
them
in
the
large
hall
of
a
boarding
school.
We
were
all
eating
ice
cream
when

Once, when I was teaching English to children in Cambridge, I died while eating my dinner.
The children were eleven to fourteen years old and there were about one hundred and
twenty
of
them
in
the
large
hall
of
a

were

eating

cream
when

items.title: I
items.content_es:
made
strange
noises
and
fell
forward
onto
my
plate.
This was not some unusual way of teaching the children about crime, but a murder mystery
game.
It
was
a
bit
like
a
play
in
the
theatre,
but
the
whole
school
was
involved.
All
of
the
teachers
were
either
victims
or
suspects
and
all
of
the
children
were
detectives
trying
to
find
out
who
the
murderer
was.
The
children
knew
the
situation
was
serious
as
many
teachers
were
“dying”,
so
they
were
all
very
excited.
Lying next to my ice cream, I loved being a “victim”. However, I had to remember not to smile
as
the
teachers
dragged
me
across
the
floor
to
get
rid
of
my
dead
body
so
the
students
knew
